mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit extensions and residential construction activity indicates robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling period, or the cross-correlation between RSAFS and HOUST breaks down without affecting SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion demonstrates supply-side resilience that outpaces softening demand, preserving profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, indicating that supply growth is purely inflationary rather than earnings-supportive.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the Federal Reserve's implicit target forces sustained monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite inflation readings consistently above 3.5%, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending, directly supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no incremental predictive power over UNRATE alone, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series confirm strength.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with higher aggregate corporate earnings, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat during quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply chain disruptions and cost-push inflation, which compress corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average during rate hikes, or the signal fails to predict margin compression in S&P 500 earnings reports.
